1→"""Unit tests for get_pr_for_plan exec command. 2→ 3→Tests GitHub issue plan-header metadata extraction and PR lookup by branch. 4→Uses FakeGitHub and FakeGitHubIssues for fast, reliable testing. 5→""" 6→ 7→import json 8→from datetime import UTC, datetime 9→from pathlib import Path 10→ 11→from click.testing import CliRunner 12→ 13→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.get_pr_for_plan import get_pr_for_plan 14→from erk_shared.context.context import ErkContext 15→from erk_shared.context.testing import context_for_test 16→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub 18→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues 19→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo 20→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo 21→from erk_shared.gateway.time.fake import FakeTime 22→from erk_shared.plan_store.draft_pr import DraftPRPlanBackend 23→ 24→ 25→def make_plan_header_body( 26→ *, 27→ branch_name: str | None = "P5103-feature-branch", 28→) -> str: 29→ """Create a test issue body with plan-header metadata block.""" 30→ branch_line = f"branch_name: {branch_name}" if branch_name is not None else "branch_name: null"

Manual edits may break erk tooling. --> 146→# Error Cases 151→ """Test error when plan-header has no branch_name field.""" 167→ assert output["error"] == "no-branch-in-plan" 172→ """Test error when branch exists but no PR for it.""" 189→ assert output["error"] == "no-pr-for-branch" 194→ """Test error when plan issue doesn't exist.""" 208→ assert output["error"] == "plan-not-found" 213→ """Test error when issue has no plan-header block.""" 231→ assert output["error"] == "no-branch-in-plan" 282→def test_json_output_structure_error() -> None: 283→ """Test JSON output structure on error.""" 299→ assert "error" in output 304→ assert isinstance(output["error"], str) 358→ # Should fail with error 362→ assert output["error"] == "no-branch-in-plan" 398→ """Test that draft-PR backend returns error when PR doesn't exist.""" 413→ # Error goes to stderr for _exit_with_error 414→ error_output = result.stderr if result.stderr else result.output 415→ output = json.loads(error_output) 417→ assert output["error"] == "no-pr-for-branch"

64:class FakeGit(Git): 65- """In-memory fake implementation of git operations. 66- 67- State Management: 68- ----------------- 69- This fake maintains mutable state to simulate git's stateful behavior. 70- Operations like add_worktree, checkout_branch modify internal state. 71- State changes are visible to subsequent method calls within the same test. 72- 73- When to Use Mutation: 74- -------------------- 75- - Operations that simulate stateful external systems (git, databases) 76- - When tests need to verify sequences of operations 77- - When simulating side effects visible to production code 78- 79- Constructor Injection: 80- --------------------- 81- All INITIAL state is provided via constructor (immutable after construction). 82- Runtime mutations occur through operation methods. 83- Tests should construct fakes with complete initial state. 84- 85- Mutation Tracking: 86- ----------------- 87- This fake tracks mutations for test assertions via read-only properties: 88- - deleted_branches: Branches deleted via delete_branch() 89- - added_worktrees: Worktrees added via add_worktree() 90- - removed_worktrees: Worktrees removed via remove_worktree() 91- - checked_out_branches: Branches checked out via checkout_branch() 92- 93- Examples:
... [1 lines omitted] ...

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: Run all applicable code reviews locally against the current branch 3→--- 4→ 5→# /local:code-review 6→ 7→Run all applicable code reviews locally, replicating the CI review experience from `.github/workflows/code-reviews.yml`. Discovers changed files, matches review definitions, runs matching reviews in parallel, and presents a unified report. 8→ 9→## Instructions 10→ 11→### Phase 1: Discover Changed Files and Match Reviews 12→ 13→**Get changed files:** 14→ 15→```bash 16→git diff --name-only $(git merge-base master HEAD)...HEAD 17→``` 18→ 19→If no changed files, report "No changes found relative to master" and stop. 20→ 21→**Read review definitions:** 22→ 23→Read the frontmatter (first `---` block) of each `.erk/reviews/*.md` file. Extract: 24→ 25→- `name`: Review name 26→- `paths`: List of glob patterns (gitignore-style, e.g., `**/*.py`, `src/**/*.py`) 27→- `enabled`: Whether the review is active (skip if `false`) 28→- `model`: Which model to use 29→ 30→**Match reviews to changed files:**
... [104 lines omitted] ...
104→**Verify output files exist** before reading. If any are missing, report which review failed.

1→# Convention-based code reviews 2→# Add a new review by dropping a markdown file in .erk/reviews/ 3→name: code-reviews 4→ 5→on: 6→ pull_request: 7→ types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review] 8→ 9→jobs: 10→ discover: 11→ if: vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false' && github.event.pull_request.draft != true && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'erk-plan-review') 12→ runs-on: ubuntu-latest 13→ timeout-minutes: 5 14→ outputs: 15→ matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }} 16→ has_reviews: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.has_reviews }} 17→ permissions: 18→ contents: read 19→ pull-requests: read 20→ 21→ steps: 22→ - name: Checkout repository 23→ uses: actions/checkout@v4 24→ with: 25→ fetch-depth: 1 26→ 27→ - name: Install uv 28→ uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v5 29→ 30→ - name: Install erk

session.assistant: Now I need to update the `has_reviews` output to account for the skip, and the discover step's env/run sections need the condition too. Let me read the current state.
session.tool_result:
1→# Convention-based code reviews 2→# Add a new review by dropping a markdown file in .erk/reviews/ 3→name: code-reviews 4→ 5→on: 6→ pull_request: 7→ types: [opened, synchronize, ready_for_review] 8→ 9→jobs: 10→ discover: 11→ if: vars.CLAUDE_ENABLED != 'false' && github.event.pull_request.draft != true && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'erk-plan-review') 12→ runs-on: ubuntu-latest 13→ timeout-minutes: 5 14→ outputs: 15→ matrix: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.matrix }} 16→ has_reviews: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.has_reviews }} 17→ permissions: 18→ contents: read 19→ pull-requests: read 20→ 21→ steps: 22→ - name: Check local review marker 23→ id: check_local_review 24→ env: 25→ GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }} 26→ run: | 27→ HEAD_SHA="${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}" 28→ MARKER="<!-- erk:local-review-passed:${HEAD_SHA} -->" 29→ PR_BODY=$(gh pr view ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} \ 30→ --repo ${{ github.repository }} --json body -q .body)
... [98 lines omitted] ...
66→ echo "Discovery failed"
session.assistant: Now update the `has_reviews` output to account for the skip. When skip is true, the discover step doesn't run, so `has_reviews` is empty/unset. The `review` job already checks `has_reviews == 'true'`, which won't match when the discover step is skipped (outputs are empty). This means the review job won't run when local review marker is found. This is correct behavior - no further changes needed for the `has_reviews` output.

session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:
## Summary
### Changes Made
1. **New exec script: `set-local-review-marker`** (`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/set_local_review_marker.py`) - Auto-detects current branch and HEAD SHA via gateway injection - Finds PR for the branch using `get_pr_for_branch` - Strips any existing marker from PR body, appends new one with current SHA - Marker format: `<!-- erk:local-review-passed:<sha> -->` - Graceful handling for no-PR, detached HEAD cases (exits 0 with JSON reason)
2. **Registered in exec group** (`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py`) - Added import and `add_command` registration as `set-local-review-marker`
3. **Updated local code-review command** (`.claude/commands/local/code-review.md`) - Added Phase 4: Mark PR as Reviewed (only runs when 0 violations) - Reports marker status to the user
4. **Updated CI workflow** (`.github/workflows/code-reviews.yml`) - Added `Check local review marker` step as first step in `discover` job - Compares marker SHA against PR head SHA - All subsequent steps gated with `if: steps.check_local_review.outputs.skip != 'true'` - When marker matches, entire review pipeline is skipped
5. **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_set_local_review_marker.py`) - 10 tests covering: happy path, no PR, existing marker replacement, PR with footer, detached HEAD, helper functions
6. **Regenerated exec reference docs** (`.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md`)
